title: Internal artifact and container registry for air-gapped environments
description: Deploys an internal OCI-compatible container registry and language artifact repository (Maven, PyPI, npm mirror) with promotion workflows that enforce provenance and signing. Enables reproducible, verifiable builds in environments with no outbound internet access.

cost.costNotes: Usually incompatible with $0 opex unless the registry is a shared enterprise service.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min ($200): Small-scale setup with 1-2 VMs (2 vCPU, 8GB RAM @ $80-100/month), 500GB storage ($50-60/month), basic backup ($30-40/month), minimal bandwidth costs ($20-30/month). Suitable for small teams with limited artifacts. Max ($8000): Enterprise-grade HA cluster with 6+ VMs for registry nodes and load balancers ($3000-4000/month), 10-20TB storage with replication ($2000-3000/month), enterprise backup solution with retention ($1000-1500/month), network infrastructure and bandwidth for air-gapped replication ($800-1200/month), monitoring/logging infrastructure ($200-300/month). The range accounts for hardware procurement/leasing in on-prem environments, storage growth, and disaster recovery requirements.
provenance.adoptionCost: High complexity implementation requiring: (1) Registry platform selection and deployment (Harbor, Nexus, or JFrog Artifactory) - 40-60 hours of evaluation and setup; (2) Air-gap promotion workflow design with vulnerability scanning, signing, and approval gates - 60-80 hours; (3) Integration with existing CI/CD pipelines for multiple language ecosystems - 40-60 hours; (4) Security hardening, access controls, and compliance documentation for isolated environments - 40-50 hours; (5) Backup/restore procedures and disaster recovery testing - 30-40 hours; (6) Team training on promotion workflows and operations - 20-30 hours. Total: 230-320 hours. At blended rate of $30-35/hour with AI assistance (senior eng + AI reducing traditional timeline by 40-50%), this yields $7000-9000. Complexity drivers include multi-format artifact support (OCI, npm, Maven, PyPI, etc.), air-gap constraints requiring manual replication processes, and governance requirements typical of secure environments.
provenance.licenseCost: Commercial enterprise registry licenses for air-gapped environments typically required for support and security features. Harbor (open source) = $0, but lacks enterprise support. JFrog Artifactory Pro (self-hosted) = $8,000-15,000/year for small teams. Sonatype Nexus Repository Pro = $10,000-20,000/year. JFrog Enterprise+ for air-gap with HA = $30,000-50,000/year. Using mid-range estimate of $15,000 for a commercial solution that provides vulnerability scanning, RBAC, replication, and vendor support - critical for air-gapped security compliance. Many organizations choose commercial over OSS for air-gapped use cases due to liability and support requirements. This is annual licensing amortized as one-time adoption cost.

provides.reasoning: This pattern explicitly provides an internal registry for storing and managing artifacts (OCI containers and language-specific packages), which is the core function of an artifact registry.
The pattern specifically supports OCI containers as part of its artifact storage capabilities, providing a dedicated container/Docker image registry function.
The pattern enforces promotion workflows and provenance tracking, which are essential supply chain security controls for ensuring artifact integrity and traceability in air-gapped environments.
The pattern explicitly enforces promotion workflows as a core feature, managing the progression of artifacts through different stages or environments with controlled gates.

requires.reasoning: An artifact registry requires persistent storage to store container images, artifacts, and metadata across restarts and ensure durability in an air-gapped environment.
Registry access needs authentication to control who can push/pull artifacts and enforce security policies, especially critical in air-gapped environments.
Fine-grained authorization is needed to implement promotion workflows and control which users/services can access or promote artifacts through different stages.
Air-gapped deployments require network isolation capability to ensure the registry operates in a disconnected or highly controlled network environment, preventing unauthorized external access.
Artifact availability and disaster recovery are critical in isolated environments where external sources are unavailable, requiring backup and restore capabilities for business continuity.

supports_nfr.reason: On-prem artifact registry with HA configuration (replicated storage, load-balanced nodes) can achieve up to four nines availability.
Artifact and container registry reads from persistent storage have at least 10ms p95 latency due to storage I/O.
Large artifact downloads and storage operations result in at least 50ms p99 latency due to on-prem disk and network overhead.
Registry service recovery in air-gapped environment requires local service restart and storage remount, taking at least 5 minutes.
On-prem storage and network bandwidth limit registry to approximately 10,000 concurrent pulls per second (36M per hour ceiling).
On-prem artifact registry with persistent storage and network constraints can handle up to 50,000 artifact read requests per second.
Artifact push operations with promotion workflows and storage writes are limited to 10,000 write operations per second on-prem.
Air-gapped registry with promotion workflows requires comprehensive audit logging for supply chain security and compliance tracking.
Registry can support GDPR compliance through access controls and audit logging when necessary for EU operations.
Air-gapped registry with proper controls can meet HIPAA requirements for healthcare software supply chain management.
Registry access controls and audit trails support CCPA compliance when necessary for California-based operations.
Internal registry with controlled promotion workflows supports SOX compliance through immutable artifact storage and audit trails.
Registry can provide read-your-writes consistency for artifact pushes or operate with eventual consistency depending on configuration.
Artifacts must be durably stored with replication as they are critical dependencies for application deployments and builds.

supports_constraints.reason: Air-gapped artifact registry pattern is specifically designed for on-premises environments with network isolation requirements.
Infrastructure pattern that provisions artifact and container registry supporting all language ecosystems and package formats.
Infrastructure pattern that provides artifact storage and distribution for any platform type requiring dependencies or containers.

registry_type.description: Type of artifacts to support. oci-only: Docker/container images only. oci-and-language: Containers plus language-specific packages (npm, Maven, PyPI, etc.). language-only: Only language package managers without container support.

storage_backend.description: Underlying storage mechanism. filesystem: Local disk (simple, lower cost). s3-compatible: Object storage like MinIO (scalable, durable). block-storage: Network-attached block volumes (performance, snapshots).

promotion_workflow.description: How artifacts move between environments. manual-approval: Human review required for promotion. automated-policy: Policy-based automatic promotion (e.g., after tests pass). no-promotion: Direct push to all repositories.

vulnerability_scanning.description: Enable automated vulnerability scanning of artifacts and container images before allowing promotion or deployment.

artifact_retention_days.description: Number of days to retain old artifact versions before automatic cleanup. Longer retention increases storage costs but provides more rollback options.

replication_mode.description: Registry availability architecture. single-node: One instance (simple, lower cost). active-passive: Primary with standby replica (HA with failover). active-active: Multiple active nodes (highest availability and performance).

warn_nfr.reason: At <1 req/s, a shared external registry or simple file server may be more cost-effective than maintaining a dedicated on-prem artifact registry infrastructure.
warn_nfr.message: onprem-airgapped-artifact-registry: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (<1). For very low traffic, simpler artifact distribution methods may be more appropriate than a full registry.

warn_nfr.reason: At <0.1 write req/s (roughly one artifact push every 10 seconds), the operational overhead of running and maintaining an on-prem registry with promotion workflows may outweigh benefits.
warn_nfr.message: onprem-airgapped-artifact-registry: peak write QPS is {actual} req/s (<0.1). With infrequent artifact pushes, simpler artifact storage solutions may be more cost-effective.

warn_nfr.reason: Air-gapped artifact registry requires HA infrastructure (replicated storage, load balancers). Very low availability targets (<95%) suggest this infrastructure investment may not be necessary.
warn_nfr.message: onprem-airgapped-artifact-registry: availability target is {actual} (<0.95). For low availability requirements, simpler single-node artifact storage may be more appropriate than HA registry infrastructure.
